title: poa arctica subsp. aperta
intro: Poa arctica subsp. aperta, also known as Arctic bluegrass, is a perennial grass species that grows in the Arctic tundra regions of the world. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Poa arctica subsp. aperta include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
